title: Psephellus marschallianus
taxonomy: Psephellus marschallianus belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification places Psephellus marschallianus within the broader category of dicotyledonous plants. At the genus level, Psephellus marschallianus is a member of the genus Psephellus, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region and surrounding areas. Further classification includes the subfamily Carduoideae, which consists of thistle-like plants. Within the Carduoideae subfamily, Psephellus marschallianus is further classified as a species within the tribe Cardueae.

intro: Psephellus marschallianus, also known as Marschall's dandelion or dandelion chicory, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of digestion, and support of liver function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Psephellus marschallianus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and polyphenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in herbal supplements and capsules, which are commonly available in health food stores and online.
